title: Insertion Sort
description: Implement Insertion Sort. Given an array of integers, sort it in ascending order using insertion sort. Return a 2D array where each row is the state of the array after each insertion step.
key_concept: Shift elements right to insert current element in its sorted position — O(n²) but O(1) space, stable, efficient for nearly sorted arrays
when_best: Nearly sorted input, small arrays, or as a subroutine in Timsort

hints.L1: Iterate from index 1. For each element, compare backward and shift right until you find its correct position.
hints.L2: Inner while loop: while j > 0 and arr[j-1] > arr[j]: swap, j--. Capture state after each outer iteration.
hints.L3: for i in 1..n: j=i; while j>0 and arr[j-1]>arr[j]: swap(arr,j,j-1); j--; record arr snapshot
